source_quote: the beacon by which the sailors steered

name: Pip perceives the beacon’s ugly appearance up close
source_quote: like an unhooped cask upon a pole,—an ugly thing when you were near it

name: Pip recognizes the other object as a gibbet that held a pirate
source_quote: the other, a gibbet, with some chains hanging to it which had once held a pirate
location: edge of the river / at the gibbet

name: Man limps toward the gibbet
source_quote: The man was limping on towards this latter
location: marshes near the river, towards the gibbet

name: Pip imagines the man as the pirate come back to life
source_quote: as if he were the pirate come to life
time: as the man limps towards the gibbet

name: Pip imagines the pirate coming down from the gibbet
source_quote: as if he were the pirate come to life, and come down

name: Pip imagines the pirate going back to hook himself up again
source_quote: and going back to hook himself up again

name: Philip Pirrip feels a terrible shock at his thought
source_quote: It gave me a terrible turn when I thought so
time: when Philip Pirrip has the thought

name: Philip Pirrip has a frightening thought
source_quote: when I thought so
time: immediately before feeling the terrible turn

name: Philip Pirrip sees cattle lifting their heads
source_quote: as I saw the cattle lifting their heads to gaze after him
time: immediately after Philip Pirrip’s frightening thought

name: cattle lift their heads and gaze after the horrible young man
source_quote: the cattle lifting their heads to gaze after him
time: immediately after the horrible young man passes by

name: Philip Pirrip wonders if the cattle share his thought
source_quote: I wondered whether they thought so too
time: while Philip Pirrip watches the cattle

name: Philip Pirrip looks all around for the horrible young man
source_quote: I looked all round for the horrible young man
time: after seeing the cattle and wondering about their thoughts

name: Philip Pirrip fails to see any sign of the horrible young man
source_quote: and could see no signs of him

name: Philip Pirrip becomes frightened again
source_quote: But now I was frightened again
time: after failing to see the horrible young man

name: Philip Pirrip runs home without stopping
source_quote: and ran home without stopping.
location: from the outdoor location to Philip Pirrip's home
